instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/tax/what-is-form-26as.html
Download Form 26AS through TRACES
In a financial year, there are several tax deductions from an account. As a taxpayer, you, too, may be curious to get this consolidated detail in one place rather than digging through various websites and documents. Form 26AS is the document you may need for this. Form 26AS is a comprehensive document containing all the year's tax-related information. It shows details based on the linked PAN card.¹ Read More
What is Form 26AS?
Form 26AS is an official statement document that mentions all the tax-related transactions linked with a PAN Card. It also provides information on advance tax and self-assessment tax payments, along with details of any high-value transactions. You can easily download Form 26AS from the TRACES website. ¹
Form 26AS can also be viewed when you wish to know if the TDS deducted from your account was deposited to the income tax department or not. ¹ The form contains transactions like TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) and/ or TCS (Tax Collected at Source).
The updated form now also contains details of mutual fund purchases, foreign remittances, refund details, dividends, etc. ¹
What does Form 26AS contain?
Form 26AS majorly contains tax-related transaction details of a PAN Card. The following details are usually in the Form 26AS: ¹
TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) transaction detailsDetails of TCS (Tax Collected at Source) transactionsAny advance tax that is paidTax payment by self-assessmentRegular assessment tax paidAll the details of the refund received from the income tax on the PAN Card in a financial yearAny major transaction like shares purchase or sale, mutual fund transactions, etc.Tax deducted on sale of any immovable propertyTDS defaults in the financial yearDetails of turnover as mentioned in GSTR-3BAny specified financial transactionCompleted and pending proceedings of Income tax.
Structure of Form 26AS: 2022-2023 onwards
As discussed above, the form now also contains details of mutual funds, dividends, foreign remittances, etc. Here is a glance at the different structures in Form 26AS: ¹
Part ITransaction information of Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) on business, interest income, salary, profession, etc. ¹
Part IITransaction description of Tax Deducted at Source (15G/15H). This is usually for senior citizens who have income less than the exemption limit. ¹
Part IIIThis part has transaction details under Proviso to the IT section 194B/First Proviso to its sub-section (1) of the IT section 194R/ Proviso to its sub-section(1) of IT section 194S. ¹
It also collects details of TDS on payments done in kind like lottery prizes, etc.
Part IVTDS details under Sections 194IA/ 194IB / 194M/ 194S (Property's landlord/Seller/Professionals/Contractors/ Seller of Virtual Digital Asset). ¹
TDS is deducted if the house rent exceeds ₹50,000 per month, payment is made to a professional or contractor above ₹50 lakhs, and virtual digital assets. Virtual digital assets transactions attract 1% TDS, and for those without a PAN card, TDS is 20%2
Part VPart V has transaction details under Proviso to sub-section(1) of the IT section 194S as per the Form-26QE Virtual Digital Asset seller) ¹
Part VIAny TCS transaction details under Section 206C¹
Part VIIPart VII has details of refunds¹
Part VIIIThis part has TDS details applicable under Section 194IA/ 194IB /194M/194S (Concerned with the buyer of a property, tenant, payments made to contractors/professionals, or buyers of virtual digital assets ) ¹
Part IXThis part has transaction/demand payment details under Proviso to sub-section(1) of section 194S as per Form 26QE¹
Part XDefault payments for TDS/TCS¹
Steps to download Form 26AS
Taxpayers can access Form 26AS to view or download it either through the TRACES platform. To download Form 26AS, you need to follow the following steps¹:
Step 1Visit the official e filling website.
Step 2Enter log in details, enter the user id which is usually the PAN or Aadhaar number. Enter the password and continue.
Step 3On the screen, click on e-file and then choose the option ‘Income Tax Returns’.
Step 4Here, click on the drop-down menu and choose ‘Form 26AS’.
Step 5Now, click on “Confirm,” after which you will be redirected to the TRACES website.
Step 6On the TRACES website, click “Proceed” on the box that appears.
Step 7Scroll to the end of the page and click on the ‘View Tax Credit (Form 26AS)’. It will show you your Form 26AS.
Step 8Next, enter the year of assessment you want Form 26AS for and the format. To download it, choose pdf format.
Step 9Submit the verification code and click on the ‘View/Download’ option.
Once downloaded, Form 26AS can be viewed or you can print it for further requirements.
Conclusion
Form 26AS is an essential document for a taxpayer. Whether you want to check the update of your TDS or TCS payment or you need it for tax filing, Form 26AS plays a crucial role. By following the simple steps, you can easily download or view the Form 26AS associated with your PAN Card.
FAQs
What is Form 26AS?Form 26AS is a document that contains details of the tax-related transaction associated with a PAN Card. It contains details of TCS and Tax Deducted at Source payments along with high-value transactions like cryptocurrency, mutual funds, dividends, etc.
Can I view Form 26AS through net banking?Yes, several banks allow you to view Form 26AS through their Internet banking facility. The list of these banks is: ¹
Citibank N.A.The Saraswat Co-operative Bank LimitedState Bank of PatialaUCO BankBank of IndiaAxis Bank LimitedOriental Bank of CommerceIDBI Bank LimitedIndian Overseas BankState Bank of TravancoreCity Union Bank LimitedCorporation BankKarnataka BankThe Federal Bank LimitedUnion Bank of IndiaState Bank of IndiaKotak Mahindra Bank LimitedBank of BarodaIndian BankICICI Bank LimitedBank of MaharashtraState Bank of MysoreReferences:
https://cleartax.in/s/view-form-26as-tax-credit-statementhttps://economictimes.indiatimes.com/wealth/tax/how-tds-on-crypto-virtual-digital-assets-will-work-from-july-1-2022/articleshow/92409625.cms
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable. While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) assumes no liability for its accuracy. The opinions expressed do not constitute formal recommendations, professional advice, or definitive guidance. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own due diligence and are advised to seek independent professional or expert advice before making any financial or investment decisions based on the content. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.
---------------------------------------------------------
